RT by @AmazingCreditC: Finally got the SBI Cashback Card 🎉

Well... not directly 😅

After holding 15+ credit cards, SBI wasn't too excited about adding another one to my portfolio. So I got it as an add-on card instead.

Looks like SBI really doesn't like people holding too many cards.

But there's a reason why this card is called the DON of Cashback Cards 👇

💳 SBI Cashback Card

💰 Fees
• Joining Fee: ₹999 + GST
• Annual Fee: ₹999 + GST
• Fee waiver on ₹2L annual spends
🔥 Cashback Structure
✅ 5% cashback on eligible online spends
No merchant restrictions.

Whether it's:
• Amazon
• Flipkart
• Myntra
• Brand websites
• Most online purchases

You earn 5% cashback (subject to exclusions).

✅ 1% cashback on eligible offline spends

📈 Current Cashback Cap
• Online cashback capped at ₹2,000 per statement cycle
• Offline cashback capped at ₹2,000 per statement cycle
• Maximum total cashback: ₹4,000 per statement cycle
Quick math:
₹40,000 eligible online spends/month = ₹2,000 cashback

💸 Cashback Credit
No reward points.
No redemption headaches.

Cashback is automatically credited to the SBI Card account after statement generation.

⛽ Fuel Benefit

• 1% fuel surcharge waiver
• Transactions between ₹500 and ₹3,000
• Maximum waiver of ₹100 per statement cycle

⚠️ Major Exclusions
No cashback on:
• Utilities
• Insurance
• Fuel
• Rent
• Wallet loads
• Education fees
• Jewellery
• Railways
• EMI transactions
• Government spends
• Toll payments
• Digital gaming

🎯 My favourite use case

Buying discounted Gift Vouchers from platforms like CRED and other voucher stores and then earning an additional 5% cashback on top.

That's where this card really shines.

No lounge access.
No fancy milestones.
No premium metal construction.

Just pure cashback.
And sometimes that's all you need.

items.title:
😜 Another unpopular opinion…

My Infinia hasn’t earned a place in my wallet for last 3 years — forget using it for show-off! 😅

I have been using it only for SmartBuy ever since I got my Magnus in 2023.

💳 2023 to Sep’24: Magnus was giving me:
✅ ~4.8% miles on spends
✅ 25K milestone points

That was significantly better than Infinia’s ~3.3% reward rate.

✈️ Post Sep’24: I shifted most spends to Atlas, which was offering:
✅ 4% miles on spends
✅ Additional milestone bonuses

Again, comfortably ahead of Infinia’s ~3.33% return.

🌍 Currently: Most of my spends are going on HSBC Premier Credit Card:
✅ 3% miles on almost all spends
✅ No major MCC exclusions (except fuel)
✅ Equivalent to ~6.6% value when redeemed for Accor stays

Don’t get me wrong — Infinia is an excellent card for SmartBuy. 👍

But for regular online and offline spending, I personally find it below average compared to several alternatives available over the last few years. Even its domestic lounge proposition is fairly ordinary now.

So for me, there is simply no reason to keep Infinia in my wallet. It stays at home in drawer. Card details are already saved on PayZapp SmartBuy, no need to bring that out even once a month from the drawer. 😄

✈️ 8 Domestic Airport Lounge Visits every year (2 per quarter)

items.title:
💳✨ After the entry of metal credit cards a few years back, we have seen very little innovation in the appearance of credit cards.

And then, last month, CheQ in partnership with AU Small Finance Bank launched a credit card with a very unique feature — a working LED in the credit card that shines with every successful swipe or tap transaction 💡.

👉The LED version comes with a one-time fee of ₹1,179 (including GST), in addition to the regular annual fee of ₹589 (including GST). There is also a non-LED version where this one-time fee is not charged. Of course, customers have the option to choose.

🗓️But as an introductory offer, the LED card is being offered FREE if applied till 15th June 2026 🎉 (no additional fee for LED)

Plus ➕ FREE EazyDiner Prime membership — all at zero joining cost 🍽️

⚡ Reward rates: While LED is a very new and interesting feature, when I looked at the card’s benefits, it actually comes across as a useful daily spend credit card at a nominal fee. 

Let’s look at its reward structure 👇

🛒 12% rewards on Amazon, Flipkart, Zomato, BigBasket, Apple (Unicorn), CheQ Travel flights/hotels & utility bill payments (electricity, gas, water, mobile recharge, FASTag) (= 6% Valueback in rupee terms)

🛍️ 5% rewards on all online shopping, food delivery, online grocery & travel bookings — covering 10,000+ brands. (= 2.5% Valueback in rupee terms)

📲 2.5% rewards on UPI payments via CheQ App  (= 1.25% Valueback in rupee terms)

💸 1% unlimited rewards on all other eligible spends ( (= 0.5% Valueback in rupee terms)

⚠️ 12% & 5% rewards are capped at 12,000 CheQ Points/month (= ₹6,000 max value/month at best redemption rate)

⚠️ Rewards calculated on ₹200 & multiples

⚠️ No rewards on fuel, rent, wallet loads, insurance, education, EMI, gold & government payments

💰 Rewards are given in the form of CheQ Points, which can be redeemed against flights/hotel bookings or gift card purchases on CheQ App.

➡️ 2 CheQ Points = ₹1 value at best redemption

CheQ also claims lowest flight prices on their app — and compensates if you find a cheaper rate elsewhere ✈️🙌

🧾 If you want, these CheQ Points can also be converted into real cashback as statement credit at: 
➡️ 4 CheQ Points = ₹1 

💳 Annual fee: ₹589 (including GST)

✅ Fee waived on ₹3 lakh annual spend

My verdict 👇

This is a very interesting credit card. While the LED is definitely a unique feature💡, the reward structure is also decent. 

✅✅✅ It offers good valueback on daily spends, especially utility bills, recharges, travel and India’s most popular e-commerce apps like Amazon, Flipkart, BigBasket etc.  This covers significant part of daily spend and 6% on that is a good reward rate.

On top of that, 1.25% valueback on UPI is great.

Overall, the reward rate quite useful for a card with just a ₹589 annual fee.

If you are looking for a low-fee card with good reward rates for day-to-day spending, this looks like a good option 👍

👉 In case you want to apply for AU Credit Card, here is the link:
https://cheq.onelink.me/3UIC/8d3zyrmp

items.title:
Many people value all airmiles at ₹1 per mile. However, when you need to travel during peak season—when airfares can skyrocket—airmiles can deliver substantially higher value. 💰✈️

The key point? 👇

While flight prices are highly dynamic and constantly change based on demand, the number of airmiles required for an award seat usually remains unchanged, especially when booked several months in advance. 🎯

Recently, I booked 6 Cathay Pacific seats from Mumbai to Hong Kong using:

🎯 1,56,000 Asia Miles + ₹1,36,487 (including forex markup)

(There was zero award availability on Qatar Airways for these dates.)

For the exact same flights, the cash price on Cathay Pacific’s website was:

💰 ₹3,93,880 for 6 passengers

Interestingly, prices on Indian travel aggregators were even higher. On MMT, even after coupons and card discounts, the fare was showing ₹5,22,168 🤯

Let’s ignore that and compare against Cathay Pacific’s direct pricing.

The Math 👇

💰 Cash Fare: ₹3,93,880
💳 Taxes & charges paid on award booking: ₹1,36,487

➡️ Value replaced by miles = ₹2,57,393

Therefore:

✅ 1,56,000 Asia Miles = ₹2,57,393

✅ 1 Asia Mile = ₹1.65

That’s substantially higher than the commonly assumed value of ₹1 per mile. 🚀

But it gets even better…

These Asia Miles were generated by transferring Amex Membership Rewards (MR) Points at a 2:1 transfer ratio.

So:

👉 2 Amex MR Points = 1 Asia Mile = ₹1.65

Which means:

✅ 1 Amex MR Point = ₹0.82

That’s significantly higher than the ₹0.50 valuation at which most people typically value Amex MR Points. 🎉

Of course, airmile valuations vary depending on the route, season, airline, cabin class, and prevailing cash fares. Not every redemption will generate such value.

However, if you travel, then transferable credit card points and redeeming them strategically can unlock tremendous value, especially during peak travel periods when cash fares become expensive and many time out of our budget. 🌏✈️

👉Have you ever achieved more than ₹1.50 per mile on an Economy Class redemption? 🤔

Share your best redemption story in the comments! 👇✈️

items.title:
✈️💳 Why HSBC Premier is one of the most simple airmiles-based credit cards to have?

Here are a few under-appreciated benefits of the HSBC Premier Credit Card 👇

1️⃣ Every spend is included (except fuel)

No need to apply your mind over MCCs.

There is a ₹1 lakh monthly spend limit to earn points on certain categories such as utilities, tax, rent & real estate, insurance, jewellery, e-wallets, etc.

But still, ₹1 lakh per month is a pretty good limit. 👍

2️⃣ No milestone-linked benefits, not even for fee waiver

That’s the biggest relief. 😌

Milestones are arguably the most prominent reason due to which credit card users end up overspending.

A lot of mental maths, planning and strategy goes into achieving milestone-based rewards before the deadline.

In that way, HSBC Premier gives you complete peace of mind.

3️⃣ Every penny counts for rewards

While almost all airmiles-based credit cards credit reward points on spends in multiples of ₹100, ₹150 or ₹200, HSBC cards consider every single rupee of your spend for reward points.

While you earn 3 points per ₹100 spend with HSBC Premier Credit Card, if there is any residual spend or a transaction is less than ₹100, they add this spend amount to your next transaction for rewards calculation.

Wow, itna kaun karta hai! 😃

4️⃣ Reward points are credited within 2–3 days of the transaction and can be redeemed immediately ⚡

No need to wait for statement generation.

5️⃣ You can see your entire reward points statement in the app itself 📱

Better transparency and easier tracking of points earned on every transaction.

6️⃣ Points are transferred to all airline and hotel partners almost instantly ✈️🏨

No need to wait for days to see the points in your airline or hotel account.

For an airmiles-based card, instant transfer is a very big advantage as award seats can disappear anytime while you wait for miles to reflect in your account.

7️⃣ No limit on points transfer to any airline or hotel partner

No need to track Group A, Group B, transfer caps 😃 

8️⃣ One of the lowest forex mark-ups among popular premium credit cards 🌍

Most popular premium credit cards like Infinia, EPM and Magnus charge 2.36% forex markup. Atlas charges 4.13%.

HSBC Premier charges just 1.17% forex mark-up and gives unrestricted rewards on forex spends.

Irrespective of net returns in terms of points earned, HSBC Premier reduces real cash outflow due to its low forex markup.

9️⃣ HSBC Premier reward points never expire ♾️. While most premium cards provide a 3-year window to use points and expiry is generally not an issue, no expiry of points further adds to the simplicity of this credit card.

✨ Sometimes, simplicity itself is the biggest benefit.

And HSBC Premier does a great job of keeping things simple, transparent and rewarding.

#creditcards
